sources.label: Linear hierarchy redesign
borrowed: Use alignment and restrained chrome to carry hierarchy in dense list views
Make list, panel, and header spacing feel deliberate rather than dashboard-like
rejected: Desktop productivity chrome that would over-complicate a patient booking flow

sources.label: Linear calmer refresh
borrowed: Prune controls until one dominant action remains obvious
Compact tabs and headers instead of spreading controls across the full width
rejected: Header splitting that would turn the booking stage into a workspace shell

sources.label: Carbon empty states
borrowed: Empty or degraded states should explain the condition and the next productive action
Replace the absent content with the state explanation instead of leaving structural noise behind
rejected: Illustrative filler graphics with no operational meaning

sources.label: Carbon loading
borrowed: Keep loading and refresh communication specific to the region that is settling
Use lightweight progress language instead of full-page suspense theater
rejected: Skeleton-heavy loading that would overwrite preserved booking context

sources.label: Carbon data table usage
borrowed: Give dense result rows enough breathing room to scan without truncation
Use row hover and progressive disclosure to help users scan structured data
rejected: Turning the slot browser into a true table or spreadsheet-like control

sources.label: NHS buttons
borrowed: Sentence-case action labels
Verb-first button copy that describes the action being taken
rejected: Multiple competing primary buttons in one cluster

sources.label: NHS writing guidance
borrowed: Plain English transactional sentences
Short, active-voice copy for no-supply and recovery states
rejected: Long explanatory paragraphs when one operational sentence is enough

sources.label: NHS punctuation and sentence case
borrowed: Sentence case for headings, chips, and support language
Short lists for grouped status cues

sources.label: WAI disclosure pattern
borrowed: Buttons with aria-expanded and aria-controls for row details
Enter and Space as the disclosure activation model
rejected: Inventing a custom disclosure state machine outside the platform pattern

sources.label: WCAG focus order
borrowed: Keep DOM order aligned with the meaningful reading order of ribbon, count bar, list, and support
rejected: Visual reordering that would move support or drawer controls ahead of the results narrative

sources.label: WCAG reflow
borrowed: Single-column reflow with no forced two-dimensional scrolling on narrow widths
rejected: Fixed-width row layouts that would overflow at 320 CSS pixels

sources.label: WCAG focus not obscured
borrowed: Sticky toolbars and day groups use spacing that keeps focused controls visible
rejected: Overlay behavior that would hide the active disclosure or day anchor

sources.label: WCAG consistent help
borrowed: Keep the help route in a predictable location across degraded result states
rejected: Moving help between unrelated regions as the snapshot state changes

sources.label: Playwright aria snapshots
borrowed: Scoped aria snapshots for renderable, stale, and no-supply result states
rejected: Using only pixel screenshots for accessibility proof

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
borrowed: Combine automated checks with explicit state assertions and manual-friendly artifacts
rejected: Treating axe-style automation as complete accessibility proof

sources.label: Playwright visual comparisons
borrowed: Stable screenshot capture for the proof harness
rejected: Environment-variable baselines that would not reproduce on this workstation

sources.label: Playwright screenshot stabilization
borrowed: Disable animations and hide the caret for deterministic screenshots
rejected: Screenshot capture with live transitions still running
